key_insight: HAMNO combines local convolutions, global spectral mixing, and adaptive gating in a hierarchical neural operator to better learn nonlinear, multi-scale dynamical systems, while PI-HAMNO adds physics constraints for improved stability and fidelity.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for surrogate modeling of PDE-governed engineering systems because it targets accurate long-horizon prediction, data efficiency, and physical consistency on non-periodic domains.
method: Hierarchical encoder-decoder neural operator with local convolutional and global spectral branches
Data-dependent gating to adaptively blend fine-scale and long-range information at each spatial location
Physics-informed extension using data loss plus strong-form and weak-form PDE residual penalties
evidence_signal: The models were tested on non-periodic Allen-Cahn, Cahn-Hilliard, and Swift-Hohenberg equations under long-rollout, limited-data, out-of-distribution initial conditions, and random-seed variability, where HAMNO outperformed baselines and PI-HAMNO improved stability and data efficiency.
watch_item: The weak-form physics loss relies on centroid-based tetrahedral quadrature and finite-element test functions, so implementation details and mesh/test-function choices may affect accuracy and reproducibility.
paper_title: HAMNO: A Hierarchical Adaptive Multi-scale Neural Operator with Physics-Informed Learning for Dynamical Systems
